decisions.title: Go/No-Go: Explore V2 GA Launch (March 31)
decisions.context: Multiple critical items remain before GA: amplitude event renaming (ENG-9276), legend settings persistence bug (ENG-8946), memory leaks in exports (ENG-9344), MAX_SAFE_INT warnings needed (ENG-9347). 14 days to deadline with core stability issues unresolved.
decisions.blockedBy: Memory leak investigation (ENG-9344) and telemetry standardization (ENG-9276) completion unknown

arguments.for: Feature completeness strong (families, calculated channels, compare table in flight). Delaying risks Q1 commitments and customer expectations already set.
arguments.against: Launching with memory leaks violates 'Nail the Core' principle. Data integrity bugs (ENG-9342, ENG-8946) undermine trust. Telemetry gaps block post-launch learning loop per Signal>Evidence>Decision.

decisions.title: Prioritize: Family Feature Scope for GA vs Post-GA
decisions.context: 5 family-related UX specs in design (DES-54, DES-121, DES-61) plus 3 eng tickets (ENG-9067, ENG-8601, ENG-7954). Families are core to 'Embed Deeper' but unclear what's GA-critical vs nice-to-have.

arguments.for: Families enable customers to templatize domain knowledge (moat-building). Full feature set shows differentiation vs competitors. Validates North Star by capturing recurring decision patterns.
arguments.against: Scope creep risks March 31 date. Minimum viable families (view + basic edit) may be enough for GA if post-launch iteration is fast. Design specs (DES-61 multi-alignment, DES-54 annotations) feel advanced.

decisions.title: Invest: Python Rules Infrastructure Now or Wait for Customer Pull?
decisions.context: Run-to-run rules work starting (DES-149, DES-150 candidate workflow specs). This enables automated decision capture (North Star++) but requires backend pipeline work. No customer is currently blocked.

arguments.for: 'Build the Moat' demands automation capabilities competitors lack. Rules that write themselves from analyst behavior = ultimate decision capture. Early investment enables piloting with design partners in Q2.
arguments.against: Canvas and Explore V2 still stabilizing. Premature to add third major surface area. Better to nail interactive analysis first, then automate what users actually do (Signal>Evidence). Rules without solid families/stats foundation = shaky.

decisions.title: Resolve: Data Pipeline Architecture for Scale (K2 Mission + Downsampling)
decisions.context: K2 Mission pipeline implementation starting (ENG-9104) while downsampling work continues (ENG-7920, ENG-9353 fresh data lag). These affect all products but architectural choices unclear - batch vs streaming, sampling strategies.

arguments.for: K2 represents 10x data volume vs current largest customer. Solving now validates 'Embed Deeper' at enterprise scale. Downsampling affects analysis fidelity - core to trust/moat. Architectural debt compounds.
arguments.against: K2 timeline may be flexible. Premature optimization risk - current customers aren't hitting scale limits. Downsampling heuristics need user research (which stats matter most). Engineering capacity stretched across GA blockers.

keyResults.name: Explore V2 Stability (zero P0 bugs at GA)
keyResults.current: 3 P0s open (memory leak, persistence, data integrity)

keyResults.name: Family Adoption (% customers w/ >5 families)
keyResults.current: Feature in beta - no metrics yet

keyResults.name: SSO Enterprise Readiness
keyResults.current: IdP support in progress (ENG-8157), docs auth (ENG-7447)

timeAudit.weekSummary: GA crunch mode - 90% meeting time on coordination vs execution. Engineering spread across 30+ tickets without clear critical path.
alerts: Zero time allocated for decision-making despite 4 open strategic choices
14 days to GA with no visible project mgmt artifacts (Gantt, risks, dependencies)
Design specs (DES-*) queuing up faster than eng can consume - sync needed
No dedicated time for 'why are we missing North Star metrics' root cause analysis

events.title: Family Scope Decision Meeting

events.title: Data Pipeline Architecture Review

events.title: Python Rules Investment Decision
events.description: Commit Q2 resources to automation or defer. Decision #3. Affects hiring/priorities.
